items.title: Unified API endpoint model and source provenance
items.notes: api-endpoints.jsonl is generated by run and fixture flows.

items.title: API artifact index
acceptance_criteria: api-artifact-index.json maps endpoint models to related artifacts
items.notes: api-artifact-index.json is generated by run and fixture flows.

items.title: HAR traffic ingest and redaction
items.notes: traffic.jsonl and schema artifacts are generated.

items.title: Traffic model compatibility module
items.notes: Compatibility module normalizes traffic endpoint keys and record IDs.

items.title: Request/response schema inference and schema diff
items.notes: Nested and array support exists through schema_infer.

items.title: UI field usage artifact
acceptance_criteria: ui-field-usage.jsonl generated when JS/HAR field evidence exists
items.notes: ui-field-usage.jsonl is generated from schema diff and observed field evidence.

items.title: Parameter and field semantic classifier
items.notes: Classifier feeds risk tags and invariants.

items.title: Field semantics wrapper module and missing classes
items.notes: Field semantics wrapper supports privilege_flag and filename_field classification.

items.title: Dependency graph and observed sequences
items.notes: Graph, sequence, and transition artifacts exist.

items.title: Sequence model compatibility module and value-match evidence
items.notes: Sequence wrapper exists and dependency graph includes exact redacted value matching.

items.title: Backend handler hypotheses
items.notes: Structured JSON hypotheses are generated.

items.title: Security invariant generation
items.notes: Expanded invariant taxonomy covers authorization, business logic, replay, sink, path, and response minimization checks.

items.title: Safe manual test plans
items.notes: Safe manual plans map expanded invariants to non-exploit oracle types.

items.title: Oracle result model and evaluator
items.notes: oracle evaluate converts manual oracle evidence into structured verdicts and evidence index records.

items.title: Structured LLM agent output scaffolding
items.notes: llm-agent-outputs.jsonl and schema validation scaffolding are generated without requiring live LLM calls.

items.title: Report draft and regression cases from oracle results
acceptance_criteria: confirmed oracle result generates report draft
items.notes: report from-oracle writes deterministic report drafts, Markdown, and regression cases from confirmed oracle results.

priority_judgement.P0: Actor/account context and traffic flow metadata are the highest leverage dependencies. Without A/B actors plus flow/role/tenant/state labels, tenant/ownership/role invariants and test plans are too generic.
priority_judgement.P1: HAR capture, GraphQL, business logic, and manual feedback materially improve modeling depth while preserving DONZO's safe manual-verification boundary. HAR capture should follow the P0 metadata contract so captured traffic is immediately useful.
priority_judgement.P2: WebSocket/SSE modeling is important, but should follow REST and GraphQL core because message semantics need the same actor, schema, and invariant foundations.
priority_judgement.P3: Actual LLM multi-agent calls should wait until deterministic interfaces and schemas are stable, so LLM output can be validated and evidence-linked.

items.title: A/B actor and account model
acceptance_criteria: actor model exists before invariant and test plan generation
actors can include user_A, user_B, admin, member, other_org_user, and anonymous when available
model represents role, tenant, owned resources, actor relationships, and safe credential references
invariants and safe manual test plans consume actor model context
items.notes: Actor model now supports A/B actors, role/tenant/owned-resource relationships, safe credential references, and actor-aware invariants/test plans.

items.title: traffic.jsonl actor/state/flow metadata contract
acceptance_criteria: traffic records preserve actor, role, tenant, state, flow, step, and label when supplied
request and response schema records carry enough flow metadata to rebuild sequences
metadata is validated against the actor/account model when available
raw credentials, cookies, tokens, localStorage, and sessionStorage values are never persisted
items.notes: HAR ingest, schemas, endpoint source_context, and sequence grouping now preserve role, tenant, flow, and label metadata.

items.title: GraphQL operation modeling
acceptance_criteria: operationName, operation type, variables, selected fields, node/edge IDs, and resolver-like resource/action semantics are modeled
GraphQL operations are logical endpoints instead of being collapsed into POST /graphql
GraphQL logical endpoints feed parameter classification, invariants, test plans, oracle templates, and LLM packs
items.notes: GraphQL traffic now becomes operation models, logical endpoint models, parameter classifications, invariants, manual plans, and LLM context.

items.title: Business logic flow model
acceptance_criteria: invitation, password reset, email verification, checkout/payment, coupon, refund, subscription, file sharing, approval workflow, role change, and OAuth flows can be inferred when evidence exists
state-transition invariants are generated for inferred flows
safe manual mutation strategies include skip, repeat, reorder, replay, accept-after-revoke, confirm-before-payment, and reuse-expired-token
mutation strategies remain manual and non-destructive
items.notes: Business-flow inference now emits flow artifacts, state invariants, and safe manual mutation plans for common bug bounty workflows.

items.title: Manual execution feedback graph
acceptance_criteria: humans can enter observed status, body, state, and read-back results
400/401/403/404/409/422/429/200/201/204 feedback updates dependency graph, preconditions, state graph, and oracle confidence
unsafe live exploitation is not implemented
manual feedback is redacted and evidence-linked
items.notes: Manual feedback graph now ingests redacted human observations and produces dependency/state feedback plus oracle confidence updates without live exploitation.

items.title: Playwright/Chrome HAR capture wizard
acceptance_criteria: wizard records HAR while preserving flow label, actor, role, tenant, and state metadata
raw credentials, cookies, tokens, localStorage, and sessionStorage values are never persisted in generated artifacts
items.notes: capture-har can ingest or record a user-driven flow, redact generated HAR artifacts, and write flow-manifest.jsonl plus traffic/schema artifacts.

items.title: WebSocket and SSE modeling
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket model captures connection URL, auth handshake, message type, payload schema, channel/room/project/org IDs, and server push events
SSE model captures event names, stream scope, response schema, and tenant/data leakage candidates
message and event models feed parameter classification, invariants, and safe manual test plans
items.notes: WebSocket and SSE JSON/JSONL artifacts can now be modeled into message/event and realtime logical endpoint artifacts.

items.title: Actual LLM multi-agent calls
acceptance_criteria: API Agent, Parameter Agent, Dependency Agent, Handler Agent, Invariant Agent, Test Plan Agent, Oracle Agent, and Report Agent have structured interfaces
rule-based or deterministic implementations exist first where possible
LLM output is JSON schema validated, evidence-linked, redacted, and cannot invent endpoints or fields
LLM calls are optional or fail-closed according to policy
items.notes: Deterministic API, Parameter, Dependency, Handler, Invariant, Test Plan, Oracle, and Report agent interfaces now exist with schema-friendly runs. External LLM calls remain optional/fail-closed and evidence constrained.
